event: View of the Ruins of Diocletian's Palace at Split
symbols: monumental arches — the enduring legacy and eventual ruin of imperial Roman power
Corinthian columns — classical civilization's architectural grandeur, now weathered by time
human figures amid ruins — the continuity of ordinary life within the remnants of past empire
sunlight streaming through arches — illumination and the passage of time revealing what endures
description: A watercolor depicting the interior of a grand ruined Roman peristyle or colonnade. Massive stone arches rise overhead, supported by tall columns with ornate Corinthian capitals. Through the central arch, a view opens onto further arched passageways and a multi-story building with shuttered windows and balconies. Strong sunlight pours in from the right, casting sharp shadows across the stone floor and columns.
